core_payload.content:
# Symphony Live Session Fabric

## 1. Role
Symphony Live Session Fabric owns the long-lived runtime continuity of one orchestration run. It keeps daemon footing, harness protocol, session state, and background runtime support together so a live coding session remains durable and controllable inside one workspace.

## 2. Live Session Fabric Surface
This sub-hub groups `background-agent-runtime`, `agent-daemon`, `agent-harness-protocol`, and `symphony-agent-session`. Together they define the substrate that launches, hosts, and resumes live execution without treating agent work as one-shot prompt traffic.

## 3. Operating Boundary
This sub-hub does not own work-entry selection, workflow safety policy, or retry and observability state. Those responsibilities belong to `assignment-intake`, `workflow-safety`, and `run-observability`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Symphony Live Session Fabric groups the continuity surfaces of the orchestration system: Background Agent Runtime, Agent Daemon, Agent Harness Protocol, and Symphony Agent Session. It keeps daemon footing, live session state, and protocol-driven execution together so one run can stay durable and controllable inside one workspace instead of degrading into one-shot prompt traffic. This boundary matters because repository automation needs an explicit live-execution substrate before retries, observability, or workflow law can mean anything. It gives Symphony one clear runtime fabric for persistent sessions.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted during Symphony decomposition to isolate daemon footing, harness protocol, session continuity, and background runtime support.
